# Oak Real Estate Partners
> One platform for the full life of a commercial real estate loan. Oak Real Estate Partners originates, underwrites, and services middle-market CRE credit — $2–$20 million senior-secured bridge loans today, government-backed FHA/HUD permanent financing at stabilization — all in-house, to the standard institutions require.
Oak Real Estate Partners is a vertically integrated commercial real estate credit platform focused on the $2M–$20M bridge loan segment. For sponsors, the platform means one relationship from first loan to final payoff; for insurance, foundation, and endowment partners, a disciplined origination engine at scale. Structural facts: $520M+ originated across Oak's managed funds, 33 full-cycle loans, 3 insurance-company relationships, and FHA MAP approval through Johnson Capital Multifamily, Oak's wholly owned FHA/HUD subsidiary. Every Oak loan is senior-secured, first-lien, 55–75% stabilized loan-to-value, floating rate indexed to SOFR, with 12–36 month terms — no blind pools, no hidden leverage. Detailed performance and offering information is available through the secure investor portal.
Core focus areas: commercial real estate lending, bridge loans, FHA/HUD financing, private credit, CRE credit for institutional investors, RIAs, family offices, foundations, and endowments.
## Main Pages
- [Home](https://oakrepartners.com/): One platform for the full life of a commercial real estate loan — the platform thesis, insurance-company validation, and track record at a glance.
- [About](https://oakrepartners.com/about): The origin and structure of Oak's vertically integrated platform — origination, underwriting, closing, servicing, and capital markets under one roof.
- [Lending](https://oakrepartners.com/lending): Senior-secured bridge loans, $2M–$20M, term sheets in 48 hours; five programs, one standard, with an FHA/HUD permanent-financing path at stabilization.
- [Why Oak](https://oakrepartners.com/whyoak): The market thesis and six competitive advantages of a platform purpose-built for the small-balance CRE credit segment.
- [Investing](https://oakrepartners.com/investing): Senior-secured, first-lien CRE debt with named collateral and loan-level transparency for RIAs, family offices, foundations, and endowments.
- [Leadership](https://oakrepartners.com/leadership): 150+ years of combined experience across CRE finance, capital markets, and servicing, with roles at BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Nomura, and HSBC.
- [Subsidiaries](https://oakrepartners.com/subsidiaries): Johnson Capital Multifamily, Oak's wholly owned, FHA MAP-approved platform for government-backed permanent financing.
- [Transaction Portfolio](https://oakrepartners.com/transactions): Track record of commercial real estate loans reflecting disciplined underwriting and strategic asset selection, with a focus on capital preservation.
- [Oak Institutional Credit Solutions](https://oakrepartners.com/oak-institutional-credit-solutions): Institutional investment offering for non-ERISA accounts (foundations, endowments, insurance, trust, and taxable accounts).
- [Oak Parallel Bridge Credit Fund](https://oakrepartners.com/oak-parallel-bridge-credit-fund): Institutional investment offering for ERISA accounts (public and corporate pension plans, IRAs, and qualified accounts).
## Solutions & Keywords
- Commercial real estate lending — vertically integrated CRE credit platform: origination, underwriting, closing, and servicing in-house.
- Bridge loans — senior-secured, first-lien, $2M–$20M, 12–36 month terms, SOFR-indexed floating rate, for acquisition, refinance, repositioning, and stabilization of transitional CRE assets.
- FHA/HUD financing — long-term, government-backed (FHA/HUD-insured) permanent financing for multifamily, affordable housing, senior living, and healthcare properties, through wholly owned subsidiary Johnson Capital Multifamily.
- Private credit — institutional-standard CRE credit in the $2M–$20M small-balance segment, underwritten to NAIC CM1/CM2 insurance designation standards.
- Investor access — RIA, family office, foundation, and endowment capital placed in the same senior-secured, first-lien positions as Oak's insurance counterparties; performance and offering information available through the investor portal.
## Optional
- This website does not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy any security. Offers are made only through definitive offering documents to qualified investors.
